title: Frustration
summary: 9084 Contract — supervening events, Law Reform (Frustrated Contracts) Act 1943.
learningObjectives: Define frustration and its effect on discharge.
Explain frustrating events: destruction, illegality, failure of purpose.
Analyse limits: self-induced frustration, force majeure clauses.
Apply cases: Taylor v Caldwell, Krell v Henry, Davis v Fareham.

simpleExplanation.title: When the contract becomes impossible
simpleExplanation.summary: Frustration discharges both parties when an unforeseen event makes performance radically different — without either party's fault.
steps: Commercial purpose fails — Krell v Henry.
Not frustration — Davis v Fareham (hardship ≠ frustration).

sections.content: **Frustration** (3.3.3) completes 3.3. Contrast **frustration vs breach** and note **1943 Act** recovery.
